QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_4

question.text: In the Indian forex market, what is the Net Overnight Open Position Limit (NOOPL) for banks incorporated in India?

answer.text: It should not exceed 25% of the total capital (Tier I and Tier II) of the bank
It should not exceed 10% of the total capital of the bank
It should not exceed 50% of the Tier I capital only
It is fixed at a uniform amount of Rs 500 crore for all banks

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Per RBI guidelines, the NOOPL fixed by bank boards should not exceed 25% of total capital (Tier I and Tier II). For Indian banks, this covers all branches including overseas and OBUs; for foreign banks, only Indian branches. **The Trap:** 10% and 50% are plausible-sounding limits. The uniform Rs 500 crore option confuses NOOPL with the net worth criterion for bank trading membership. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch1 and Ch8 cover NOOPL. Series VII (Securities Operations) discusses risk limits for brokers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NOOPL limits help contain systemic risk from excessive forex exposure by banks — a key prudential regulation in currency markets.
